subject: GSM Phone Maintenance & Repair
q:
Question 2
a) What is a mobile rework station?
b) Explain network service code.
Candidates' performance was poor. Less than 5% of the candidates who attempted this question scored half of the maximum marks. They could not explain network service code while only few were able to determine mobile rework station.
explanation:
Observation
The expected answers are:
a. Mobile Rework Station: This is a piece of equipment used to remove / replace / fix electronic components
on the main board of a phone. It consists of soldering iron, or/and hot air gun, power switch, regulator, display screen etc.
b. Network Service Code:
This is the first four (04) digits of the telephone number given to a
SIM card e.g. 0803 for MTN; 0802 for airtel, 0809 for etisalat, 0805 for glo, 0704 for visafone etc

subject: GSM Phone Maintenance & Repair
q:
Question 5
a) List two causes of corrupt software problems in mobile phones.
b) State two messages on the screen of a phone with corrupt software.
c) State two ways for solving corrupt software problems.
Most of the candidates could not list two causes of corrupt software problems in mobile phone, neither could they state messages displayed on the screen of a phone with corrupt software problem. They could not state ways of solving corrupt software problem as well. This is a good question but lack of practical knowledge is attributed to the poor performance of the candidates.
explanation:
Observation
The expected answers are:
a. Causes of corrupt software.
Any correct 2
- virus attack / infection
- frequent falling
- over exposure to extreme temperatures
- contact with water or other conducting solution / fluid
b. Messages displayed due to corrupt software
Any correct 2
- Phone malfunction
- contact service provider
- file error
- incomplete / incorrect IMEI
- blank screen.
c. Solutions to Corrupt software
Any correct 2
- reheat the CPU / RAM / other memory
- flash memory
- rewrite IMEI

b) State the function of each of the following codes:
(i) *#06#;

None of the candidates could give two functions of the backup battery as well as the function of code *#05# but most candidates provided correct answer to code *#06#.

Observation
The expected answers are:

Any correct 2

b. Codes
(i) *#06#: To check the IMEI of a GSM phone.
(iii) *#05#: Brings out some hidden information (like SIM blocked, enter PUK when a phone has its SIM card blocked).

q:

A subscriber inserts his SIM card into a phone. The phone displays "phone lock: enter code".

Candidates seem not to be familiar with this question. Performance of candidates was poor as most of them could not provide correct answer to the question.
explanation:
Observation
The expected answers are:
a. The phone has its security code activated and will always ask for the code whenever the phone is powered on.
b. Correction
Any correct 2
- by inputting / entering the factory default code of the phone (if not changed by the user)
- using computer to reset the code if default is changed
- using master code
- using hardware clip

three
steps by which the language of a mobile phone can be changed from German to English.

explanation:
Observation

- English language is displayed
OR

OR
- Connect the phone to a computer
- Open the software and select the phone model/series
- Select English language then ok

-analysis of the circuit board.

2
(a)

- Examine critically the PCB for possible burnt components, corrosion, breakage, dust accumulation etc.

Ask for the history of the phone from the owner/ user e.g. contact with liquid, frequent falling, wrong insertion/incompatible charger, etc.
-Software checking
Use of flashing device to read the logs of the phone.
-Analysis of the circuit board
Use of meter to test components.

BGA stencil plates: to properly align the solder ball on every bumps of the chip
Spatula: to gather the solder balls on each hole of the stencil plate
Liquid soldering flux: to make the solder balls remain firm and adhesive
PCB holder: to ensure firm grip of PCB for proper alignment of solder balls
Lacquer thinner: to clean the board
Cotton buds : to clean the board
blades: to clean the board
ESD cleaning brush: to clear the residues
Tweezers: to pick/hold tiny objects or to align pins

q:
Question 4
A mobile phone powers on by itself as soon as battery is inserted into it and switches off intermittently. State two:
Causes of the problem;
Solutions to the problem.
The questions here tested Candidates’ knowledge of troubleshooting and fault finding, Candidates’ performance was poor. Less than 3% of the candidates who attempted this question scored below half of the maximum marks. They could not state two causes of phone switches on and off intermittently and the solutions to the problems.
explanation:
Observation
The expected answers are:
(a)
Causes of phone switching on and off intermittently
Short circuit on the PCB caused by dirt, water or other conducting fluid
Corrupt memory
Virus attack
faulty power switch
Damaged SIM card
(b)
Solutions to causes identified in 4(a)
Short circuit on the PCB caused by dirt, water or other conducting fluid: wash the PCB with recommended fluid and dry it properly
Corrupt memory: flash the phone / reheat some components
Virus attack: scanning storage devices with installed antivirus programs /formatting the phone memory
Faulty power switch: Replace power switch
Damaged SIM card: change SIM card

It is done on the phone

Remove the phone immediately from the water;
Ensure it is switched off;
Do not attempt to charge it immediately;
Remove the battery, SIM card, memory card and other accessories;
Attempt to dry the phone partially by dabbing;
Ensure the phone is exposed to dry air for complete dryness before switching it on.

Power IC
PCB

Check the terminals for corrosion

Check for corrosion
If corroded, clean
If damaged, replace

explanation:
Observation
The expected answers are:
(a)
(i) Battery life span is the total number of charge cycles a rechargeable battery will
last before battery life degrades to 80% of its original fully charged capacity.
(ii)
Effects of a weak battery on a mobile phone
The phone may:
not power/turn ON;
not be able to run some/several applications easily;
reboot/restart intermittently while in use;
shutdown/switch off unexpectedly;
get too hot/overheat.
(iii)
Methods of reviving a dead mobile phone battery
By:
boosting with a booster / eliminator;
jump-starting with a 9
V
battery;
charging with a sub-charging machine;
freezing with a freezer for minimum of 12 hours.
(b)
Activities that violate safety in a mobile phone workshop
Playing / running in the workshop
Using damaged / faulty equipment / tools
Keeping surrounding untidy / wet
Not wearing / using recommended Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) when working
Using of inappropriate equipment / tools
Placing of equipment / tools in an inappropriate position / location
Touching of heated equipment / tool to feel its degree of hotness

(i) Battery life span is the total number of charge cycles a rechargeable battery will

The phone may:
not power/turn ON;
not be able to run some/several applications easily;
reboot/restart intermittently while in use;
shutdown/switch off unexpectedly;
get too hot/overheat.
(iii)
Methods of reviving a dead mobile phone battery
By:
boosting with a booster / eliminator;
jump-starting with a 9
V
battery;
charging with a sub-charging machine;
freezing with a freezer for minimum of 12 hours.

Activities that violate safety in a mobile phone workshop
Playing / running in the workshop
Using damaged / faulty equipment / tools
Keeping surrounding untidy / wet
Not wearing / using recommended Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) when working
Using of inappropriate equipment / tools
Placing of equipment / tools in an inappropriate position / location
Touching of heated equipment / tool to feel its degree of hotness

subject: GSM Phone Maintenance & Repair
q:
Question 5
(a)
A mobile phone displays check your configuration settings when a user attempts to access the internet.
(i) State two possible causes of this problem;
(ii) Give two solutions to this problem.
(b)
List in sequential order, the steps involved in servicing a mobile phone.
The Candidates that did this question performed poorly as they were not able to provide reasonable answers to the questions asked.
explanation:
Observation
The expected answers are:
(a)
(i)
Causes of ‘check your configuration settings’ error
Wrong network configuration settings
Missing / no network configuration settings
Solutions to the identified causes of the error in 5
(a)
(i)
Send appropriate code to service provider to get correct network configuration settings
Manually input the correct network configuration settings
Contact service provider to request for network configuration settings
(b)
Steps involved in servicing a mobile phone
Turn / power OFF the phone
Remove SIM card, memory card and battery (if any)
Uncouple the phone using appropriate tools/equipment
Remove delicate detachable components on the PCB
Use ESD soft brush and recommend thinner (isopropyl alcohol) to clean the PCB
Turn / power ON the rework station/hot air blower and set it to the appropriate temperature range (low heat and low air) for complete dryness of the PCB
Allow the PCB to cool and couple the phone appropriately
Turn / power ON the phone to test for functionality

7

Tools / Equipment for flashing a mobile phone
Computer system / laptop

USB Data cable / flash cable or / FBUS cable
Drivers

USB

(b)
Steps involved in flashing a mobile phone
Switch OFF the mobile phone. Then remove SIM card and memory card (if any)
Run / Launch the extracted flashing software stored on the computer.
Locate and select the compatible phone stock
ROM
/ firmware file stored on the computer.
Connect the phone to the computer using the USB data cable/boot the phone into recovery/download mode and then connect the phone to the computer using USB data cable.
Click on the download / flash button on the flashing software to install the new stock ROM / firmware file on the phone and then wait until the flashing process is completed.
Unplug the phone from the computer and allow the phone to restart / reboot. The new stock ROM / firmware file is now installed on the phone.
(c)
Risks involved in flashing a mobile phone
Any data not previously backed up would be lost
Phone may be bricked / rendered unusable / dead
It voids the warranty of the phone
(d)
Precautions to be taken while performing flashing on a mobile phone
Ensure:
that the phone stock
ROM /
firmware file selected is compatible;
that the USB data / flash cable used is in good working condition;
to install compatible mobile phone drivers on the computer to ease connection;
no interruption while the flashing process is ongoing;
the phone battery level is at least 50% charged to avoid interruption during the flashing process;
the computer system / laptop is connected to stable external power source / supply;
to use the latest version of the phone flashing software;
to use higher version of the phone stock ROM / firmware file.
